Hey guys, Saturday, time for your extra food for thought motivation. And again, these are clips that I'm going to do research for each week and find yourself. I think some of the really great moments where I was on stage, said things that really didn't have to do that much with selling, more about life in general, food for thought. Now if you're selling out there in the field today, it's Saturday, right? Hey, well, guess what? You know, make sure you go back and listen to a daily motivation from another day, right? Get yourself pumped up. But this is more just for you personally to kind of crystallize what success is about, what life is about, and how you can feel good about yourself. So check out this clip. You're going to enjoy it. And I'm not going to see you on the other side. It's going to go right to the ending. My gift to you. And you'll get one tomorrow as well. What's the most important thing in sales, really? Trust. If they don't trust you, they'll never buy from you. True or false? They got to trust you. OK? Now you can't say to a salesman, to a prospect, trust me, please, just trust, trust me. Just trust me. Just say, what the hell is this guy talking about? OK? That's what a cheap, uneducated, subpar salesman do. They try to sell with just simply emotion and hype. And they're like, just trust me, sir. Just trust me. The stock is going up. Well, just trust me. I'll fix your credit. Just trust me. Sign up for this subscription. Just trust me. Trust. And we know better. And what happens is all those limiting buying leaves, whoop, the antenna goes up and they rise up. And next thing you know, their action threshold is through the roof and you can't close people. By the way, you talk from the very beginning, the tonal patterns that you run, you're going to start lowering their action threshold right from the beginning. Because you can establish three things that are crucial, crucial, crucial, crucial to closing a sale. And number one, that the person that they're dealing with, you, are A, sharp as a tack, B, enthusiastic as hell. Now let me just dissect this for one second. Why would they want to do business with somebody who's sharp as a tack? OK? Well, because if it's about achieving their goals, who do you want working on your problem, the moron or the person who's sharp? Hello? They'll say, oh, if I come off too sharp, they're going to think I'm a sharpie? No, they want to do business with the one who's sharp. Don't kid yourself. They want to do business with the expert, the top guy, the top girl, top dog in the field. If they don't think you're sharp as a tack, you're wasting their time. Next, you're enthusiastic. Yeah. What does that mean? He must have something really, really great to offer me. That's why he's so enthusiastic. If you're not enthusiastic, what is it saying? My product sucks. My life sucks. I'm not a winner. The state that you are in, the emotional state, whether you're in a positive, pumped up, empowered state, which is a state of certainty and clarity, which is great for sales. Write this down. Uncertainty, clarity, and conviction. And you can add a couple more things. Confidence, courage. Those are the most empowering states to be in as a salesperson. The most disempowering state is confusion, uncertainty, overwhelm, boredom. Most states will kill a salesperson. But these three things, they have to be established instantly. Or you're behind the April. And why should you be a figure of authority? This is perhaps the most important one. To become authority figure to somebody. To become the foremost expert. Cuts into three things. Number one, we were taught this with this big to simply to listen to people who are in authority. People wear uniforms. Policemen, firemen, security guards. Many of them are not licensed police officers yet. You put a badge on them in a uniform. And we just say, we do what they say, go here, we go there. You go left to the left. That's what happens when we're with figures of authority. We listen to them and we follow. You want your customer, your prospect to perceive you instantly as a figure of authority. So it will start moving towards your way of thinking. You come off as a novice and not an authority figure. They're like, great, great. Let me speak to someone in charge. That's what they're thinking. Great, sounds good. Now put someone in charge on the phone. I'll talk to them and get realistic about the stuff and get serious. So these three things have to be established immediately.
